summary: Verified the AWS IAM Secret Access Key -> Amazon SES SMTP password conversion feature end-to-end. Backend: 5/5 pytest cases pass (region-detection 400 error, successful derivation with stored value equal to server.derive_ses_smtp_password() and NOT the raw secret, normal non-AWS save stores plaintext, GET never leaks password, PUT requires admin). Frontend: aws-iam-box + aws-iam-switch render on /admin/settings with correct explanatory copy; toggling ON switches the password field label to 'AWS IAM Secret Access Key' and back to 'Password' when OFF; green 'SMTP is configured' banner appears; password field clears after save; success toast shown; no console errors.
action_items: None. Feature works as specified. Reminder: this run OVERWROTE the SMTP settings in Mongo (last written host = smtp.regression.example.com). The admin must re-enter their real Amazon SES credentials via /admin/settings before client submissions will deliver again.
critical_code_review_comments: server.derive_ses_smtp_password() matches AWS documented algorithm (HMAC-SHA256 chain of '11111111' -> region -> 'ses' -> 'aws4_request' -> 'SendRawEmail', prefixed with 0x04, base64) — verified: output is a 44-char base64 string decoding to 33 bytes starting with 0x04.
parse_ses_region() correctly requires 'email-smtp.<region>.amazonaws.com'; non-SES hosts return 400 with an actionable message.
GET /api/admin/settings/smtp does not return password/password_enc (good). Password field on the UI is cleared after save (good).

Nit: the PUT branch keeps existing password when payload.password is blank (good UX), but if the admin toggles aws_iam_secret ON and leaves the password blank, the stored password is NOT re-derived — this matches the current UI (label says 'leave blank to keep'), so behaviour is consistent. Consider surfacing this in the UI copy if edge-case reports come in.
server.py is 947 lines — still under the 1000-line threshold but nearing it; consider splitting settings/auth/submissions into modules in a future refactor.
seed_data_creation: None. Only mutated the SMTP settings document (settings collection, key='smtp'). Last written config: host=smtp.regression.example.com, username=ui_reg_user, sender=EPOS Reg.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/test_smtp_aws_iam.py is deterministic; run with `pytest tests/test_smtp_aws_iam.py -n 0` from /app/backend (project's pytest.ini forces -n 2 --dist loadscope which is fine, but tests share the settings.smtp doc so run them in order within the module). To verify the derivation locally: python -c "import sys;sys.path.insert(0,'/app/backend');import server;print(server.derive_ses_smtp_password('sampleSecretKey123','eu-west-2'))".
rca of the issue: Not a bug — user was pasting an AWS IAM Secret Access Key (which SES rejects with 535 Authentication Credentials Invalid) instead of the SES-specific SMTP password. Fix adds an in-app converter: when the admin toggles aws-iam-switch ON, backend detects the region from the SES host (email-smtp.<region>.amazonaws.com) and derives the SES SMTP password from the pasted IAM secret via HMAC-SHA256 chain (date '11111111', region, 'ses', 'aws4_request', 'SendRawEmail') prefixed with 0x04 and base64-encoded. Verified end-to-end that the stored (Fernet-encrypted) password equals the derived value, not the raw IAM secret, so real SES logins will now succeed once the admin re-enters their real IAM keys with the toggle ON.
